name: Reed-Mother Ossa
role: Aging matriarch and moral center of the Greywater Stilts
voiceHints: Slow, dry, unimpressed by cities and their powers. Runs the talking-circle by saying least and meaning most. Dying and hiding it, because the town has no agreed way to replace her and she fears what the scramble will do.
knowledge: Every fault-line in the Stilts' three factions and which will crack first when she's gone
The true terms of the Heights envoy's offer and the trap folded inside the generosity
The town's founding promise — owe nothing to the aligned powers — and what it has cost to keep
wants: To leave the Stilts able to choose its own next without tearing itself apart — and to do it before her body forces the timeline.
fears: That the independence she gave her life to becomes a bargaining chip the moment she's not there to hold the circle.

reactsToReputation.fair-broker: trusts them with the succession problem
reactsToReputation.self-serving: reads them instantly, freezes them out

appearance: A heavy old woman near eighty, deep brown skin creased fine as cloth, white hair wrapped in faded indigo. Cataract-pale in one eye. Sits with a marsh-reed staff across her knees and rarely stands for anyone.
